content.problemStatement:
A balanced parenthesis string is defined as follows:


The empty string is balanced.
If PPP is balanced, (P)(P)(P) is also balanced.
If PPP and QQQ are balanced, PQPQPQ is also balanced.
You are given two even integers nnn and kkk. Find any balanced parenthesis string of length nnn that doesn't contain a balanced substring of length kkk, or claim that no such string exists.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case consists of a single line containing nnn and kkk.


Output Format
For every test case, print on a new line, any balanced parenthesis string of length nnn that doesn't contain a balanced substring of length kkk. If there doesn't exist any such string, print −1-1−1 instead.
constraints: Sum of nnn over all test cases doesn't exceed 10510^5105.
nnn and kkk are both even integers.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case, the only balanced strings of length 444 are (()) and ()(), both of which contain () as a substring.
In the second test case, (())(()) is a balanced string that doesn't contain any balanced substring of length 666.
